candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line appears separately from the selected COGS row ('Cost of Sales'), so it does not look embedded in that row. However, the row is not labeled as COGS-specific, so assigning it to COGS would be uncertain and risks misallocation/double counting.
Separate operating amortisation line, but it is not identified as related to COGS specifically. It also appears to be a component of broader D&A disclosure rather than a COGS-only amount.
Separate operating depreciation line appears outside the selected COGS row, but it is a total depreciation line rather than clearly COGS-specific. Using it for COGS_DA would be speculative.

variables.notes: A separate operating D&A block exists above finance lines, indicating D&A may be outside the selected parent expense rows. But the excerpt provides only total depreciation/amortisation, not COGS-attributable D&A. Conservative choice is to add nothing automatically to COGS pending manual allocation review.

candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line is shown separately from the selected SG&A row ('Administration/Selling/General'), so it does not appear embedded in that selected row. However, it is not labeled as SG&A-specific, so assigning it to XSGA would be uncertain.
Separate operating amortisation line, but not identified as SG&A-specific or R&D-specific. No dedicated R&D row is visible, so allocation to XSGA_DA is unclear.

Separate total operating depreciation line appears outside the selected SG&A row, but there is no indication that it belongs specifically to SG&A/overhead rather than being enterprise-wide or partly COGS-related.

variables.notes: Manually Added. The statement shows separate operating depreciation/amortisation lines, suggesting D&A may sit outside SG&A. But no row ties that D&A specifically to SG&A or to R&D, and XRD is empty. To avoid misallocation and double counting, no automatic XSGA_DA row is selected; manual review is warranted.
